title: saxifraga hirsuta subsp. hirsuta
intro: Saxifraga hirsuta subsp. hirsuta, also known as woolly saxifrage, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high content of tannins and flavonoids. Woolly saxifrage has been reported to have various health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be administered orally or applied topically for local treatment.
